 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","title":"Install release gates, observability, and operator guidance for reliability","description":"## Why
Reliability work is not complete when the code seems better locally. It is complete when regressions are hard to ship, anomalies are visible, and operators have a playbook for field failures.

## Scope
Turn the new invariants, fixture corpus, realism suites, and recovery paths into mandatory release discipline and operator guidance.

## Deliverables
- CI/release gates for real-world reliability suites
- structured observability around anomaly classification and recovery
- updated disaster-recovery and troubleshooting docs
- a non-hermetic smoke profile that exercises ambient-env and existing-workspace behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
- reliability regressions become release blockers rather than post-release surprises
- operators can diagnose and escalate failures without reverse-engineering the code","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34.054171443Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ops","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:54.326583926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:18.751657304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:28.736789160Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:53:37.692812501Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":202,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead exists to turn architectural hardening into release discipline; otherwise the new realism work will remain optional and decay over time.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:57Z"},{"id":207,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This track exists to prevent a repeat of the current situation where the suite is green but the field experience is red. The outcome has to be operational, not just architectural.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:34Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","title":"Make real-world reliability suites mandatory in CI and release verification","description":"## Purpose
Promote the real-world reliability suites from optional or ad hoc checks into mandatory gates for CI, release verification, and high-risk changes.

## Cover
- failure-corpus replay
- crash-injection suites
- long-lived-workspace stress
- doctor/recovery postcondition checks

## Acceptance Criteria
- shipping without these suites requires an explicit conscious override, not omission by default
- release verification documents the outcome of the reliability gates","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:09:00.414820908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T16:09:00.414550331Z","close_reason":"Added mandatory CI and release reliability gates with documented emergency override","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","release-gating","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:56:53.826363038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:03.126099578Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:12.811292518Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:22.300269735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:31.628692974Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:51:57.611605676Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":208,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"If the new realism suites do not gate merges and releases, they will slowly rot into optional diagnostics and we will be back where we started.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:37Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","title":"Add structured observability and audit records for anomaly and recovery paths","description":"## Purpose
Emit structured logs, audit records, and counters for anomaly classification, quarantine, recovery attempts, and post-recovery validation outcomes.

## Scope
- record what evidence was seen
- record which automatic path ran
- record whether the outcome was success, refusal, or degraded mode
- make the resulting data easy to correlate with operator reports and bug fixtures

## Acceptance Criteria
- field incidents can be mapped back to concrete anomaly and recovery states
- debugging no longer depends on sparse ad hoc stderr snippets","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:32:00.972293878Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T15:32:00.972041656Z","close_reason":"Structured reliability audit records now expose doctor anomaly classification and repair outcomes with tracing and JSON fields.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","observability","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:41.051238985Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:00.957711972Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":209,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"A system that recovers or refuses without leaving a clear audit trail is still opaque. Observability is what turns one scary field report into reproducible engineering input.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:40Z"}]}
-{"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","title":"Rewrite troubleshooting and disaster-recovery playbooks around the hardened model","description":"## Purpose
Rewrite troubleshooting and disaster-recovery docs so they match the hardened product model rather than the current optimistic or partially outdated assumptions.

## Scope
- explain anomaly classes and what they mean
- document quarantine and evidence-preservation behavior
- provide step-by-step operator flows for safe diagnosis, escalation, and recovery
- make it clear when JSONL-only workflows are safe and when they are emergency fallbacks

## Acceptance Criteria
- future operators do not have to rediscover recovery strategy from source code or chat history
- docs match the actual behavior of `doctor`, startup, sync, and repair paths","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:04.382375983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:43.282354620Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","operations","recovery"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:50.583791301Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:57:59.792267448Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:04.382375983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:09.055748700Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":210,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"This bead is about preserving hard-won reliability knowledge.  {"id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","title":"Add a non-hermetic smoke profile for ambient-env and existing-workspace regressions","description":"## Purpose
Add a smoke profile that deliberately runs against pre-existing workspaces under ambient environment conditions instead of the fully scrubbed tempdir harness.

## Scope
- preserve selected ambient env variables that influence routing and output
- exercise commands against existing `.beads` directories rather than only fresh init
- verify that common human/operator invocation patterns do not fail immediately

## Acceptance Criteria
- the project has a lightweight always-run signal for non-hermetic regressions
- bugs caused by environment inheritance or existing-workspace assumptions surface earlier than full incident reports","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:07.716630263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-12T15:51:54.330743805Z","closed_at":"2026-03-12T15:51:54.327485718Z","close_reason":"Implemented non-hermetic smoke profile and existing-workspace regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ambient-env","reliability","smoke"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:58:18.430188701Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.4.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-12T04:01:06.963277306Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:52:07.716630263Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":211,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"The current suite’s strongest blind spot is that it proves behavior in a lab. This smoke profile is meant to be cheap enough to run often while still preserving some of the messiness of real operator environments.","created_at":"2026-03-12T03:59:46Z"},{"id":223,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Starting this now. Planned scope: add a lightweight smoke profile that runs against existing .beads workspaces with selected ambient env preserved, instead of the fully scrubbed tempdir-only harness. I’m wiring it into the existing test/harness surface first so it stays cheap and always-runnable.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:27:37Z"},{"id":225,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rsei.5.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented the non-hermetic smoke coverage. Added a smoke-oriented CLI test runner that preserves a fixed allowlist of routing/output env vars, and added an always-run e2e smoke test against the metadata_custom_paths existing-workspace fixture. Verification: cargo fmt --check passed locally; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beads_rust cargo test --test e2e_basic_lifecycle e2e_non_hermetic_smoke_existing_workspace_preserves_env_sensitive_paths -- --nocapture passed; rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_beads_rust cargo check --all-targets passed. Repo-wide clippy still fails on pre-existing tests/common/scenarios.rs pedantic issues unrelated to this bead.","created_at":"2026-03-12T15:51:50Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rt7z","title":"Integrate rich output into quick commands (q, reopen, defer, undefer, count)","description":"## Commands: br q, br reopen, br defer, br undefer, br count

### Traffic Level: LOW-MEDIUM
Quick action commands that need simple confirmations.

### br q (quick create)
```
✓ Created beads_rust-abc1
  "Quick note about something"
```

### br reopen <ID>
```
✓ Reopened beads_rust-abc1
  Status: closed → open
```

### br defer <ID> [UNTIL]
```
✓ Deferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: open → deferred
  Until: 2024-02-01 (7 days)
```

### br undefer <ID>
```
✓ Undeferred beads_rust-abc1
  Status: deferred → open
```

### br count [--status STATUS]
```
╭─ Issue Counts ──────────────────────────────────────╮
│                                                     │
│  Total: 156                                         │
│  Open: 89    In Progress: 34    Closed: 33          │
│  Ready: 23   Blocked: 12   Deferred: 5              │
│                                                     │
╰─────────────────────────────────────────────────────╯
```

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
Location: tests/cli/quick_tests.rs

```rust
#[test]
fn test_q_creates_issue() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_q_result("Quick note", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("Created"));
}

#[test]
fn test_reopen_shows_status_change() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let output = render_reopen_result("test-id", &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("closed"));
    assert!(output.contains("open"));
}

#[test]
fn test_defer_shows_until_date() {
    let ctx = OutputContext::plain();
    let until = chrono::Utc::now() + chrono::Duration::days(7);
    let output = render_defer_result("test-id", until, &ctx);
    assert!(output.contains("deferred") || output.contains("Until"));
}

#[test]
fn test_count_json_unchanged() {
    let current = run_count_json();
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/count.json");
    assert_json_eq!(current, baseline);
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/quick_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Quick Commands ==="
setup_test_db

# Test q (quick create)
log_step "Testing br q"
Q_OUTPUT=$(br q "Quick task" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$Q_OUTPUT" "Created"
Q_ID=$(echo "$Q_OUTPUT" | grep -oE 'beads_rust-[a-z0-9]+' | head -1)
log_pass "br q works"

# Test count
log_step "Testing br count"
COUNT_OUTPUT=$(br count 2>&1)
assert_contains "$COUNT_OUTPUT" "Total"
log_pass "br count works"

# Test defer
log_step "Testing br defer"
DEFER_OUTPUT=$(br defer "$Q_ID" "2099-01-01" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$DEFER_OUTPUT" "deferred"
log_pass "br defer works"

# Test undefer
log_step "Testing br undefer"
UNDEFER_OUTPUT=$(br undefer "$Q_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$UNDEFER_OUTPUT" "open"
log_pass "br undefer works"

# Close and test reopen
br close "$Q_ID" 2>&1 > /dev/null
log_step "Testing br reopen"
REOPEN_OUTPUT=$(br reopen "$Q_ID" 2>&1)
assert_contains "$REOPEN_OUTPUT" "Reopened"
log_pass "br reopen works"

log_success "=== Quick commands E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

### Logging Requirements
- Log quick command type and result
- Log status changes for reopen/defer/undefer
- Log rendering mode","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T20:37:16.000283475Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:19.949044682Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:19.948988286Z","close_reason":"Rich output integrated for q, reopen, defer, undefer, count commands. Pattern follows established version/upgrade implementation.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rt7z","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-11n3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-rt7z","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-31nl","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":169,"issue_id":"beads_rust-rt7z","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Rich output implemented for q.rs, reopen.rs, defer.rs, count.rs. Files pass rustfmt validation. Full cargo check blocked by unrelated error in audit.rs (smart quotes) - notified TealCat.","created_at":"2026-01-20T20:00:10Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-rud3","title":"Do not force absent startup bool flags to false","description":"Fresh-eye startup audit found build_cli_overrides() in src/main.rs currently maps omitted bool flags like --no-db and --no-daemon to Some(false). Because CLI overrides merge last, that prevents environment or project startup config from enabling those modes unless the user passes the flag explicitly. Fix CLI override construction so absent startup bool flags stay None, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-12T01:51:36.080215737Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T16:31:50.238947360Z","closed_at":"2026-03-15T16:31:50.238187907Z","close_reason":"Already fixed: build_cli_overrides() uses 'if cli.flag { Some(true) } else { None }' for no_db, no_daemon, no_auto_flush, no_auto_import. Regression test build_overrides_omits_absent_startup_bool_flags passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -467,15 +467,20 @@ done
 
 **Solutions:**
 ```bash
-# Manual fix: edit the file
+# Capture current classification before changing the file
+br doctor --json
+br sync --status --json
+
+# Manual fix: edit the malformed line(s)
 $EDITOR .beads/issues.jsonl
 
-# Or restore from backup
+# Or restore a known-good JSONL history entry after reviewing it
 br history list
 br history restore <backup>
 
-# Skip bad lines (lossy)
-br sync --import-only --error-policy best-effort
+# Validate and import only after the JSONL is parseable
+jq -c '.' .beads/issues.jsonl >/dev/null
+br sync --import-only --json
 ```
 
 ---
@@ -511,11 +516,14 @@ br config set id.prefix=bd
 # Check sync status
 br sync --status --json
 
-# Export current state first (backup)
-br sync --flush-only
+# Inspect both sides before choosing an authority
+br --no-auto-import --allow-stale list --json
 
-# Force import (overwrites local)
+# If JSONL is authoritative, import it explicitly
 br sync --import-only --force
+
+# If SQLite is authoritative, export it explicitly
+br sync --flush-only --force
 ```
 
 ---
@@ -595,11 +603,11 @@ br sync --flush-only -vv
 # Wait and retry with timeout
 br list --lock-timeout 10000
 
-# Find locking process
+# Identify the locking process, then coordinate with its owner
 fuser .beads/beads.db
 
-# Kill if stuck (careful!)
-# fuser -k .beads/beads.db
+# Re-check workspace health after the writer finishes
+br doctor --json
 ```
 
 **Prevention:**
@@ -620,15 +628,16 @@ fuser .beads/beads.db
 # Check br version
 br version
 
-# Try automatic migration
-br doctor
+# Classify the workspace and projected repair
+br doctor --json
+br doctor --repair --dry-run --json
 
-# Manual migration (if supported)
+# Use the supported migration path if available
 br upgrade --migrate-db
 
-# Last resort: reinitialize
-mv .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.backup
-br sync --import-only
+# Otherwise preserve evidence and escalate with the doctor output
+br where --json
+br config list -v
 ```
 
 ---
@@ -657,25 +666,21 @@ br sync --import-only
 
 **Diagnosis:**
 ```bash
-# Check integrity
-sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "PRAGMA integrity_check;"
+# Classify corruption and related sidecar/metadata state
+br doctor --json
 
-# Check for missing tables
-sqlite3 .beads/beads.db ".tables"
+# Preview the repair plan without changing files
+br doctor --repair --dry-run --json
 ```
 
 **Recovery:**
 ```bash
-# Backup current state
-cp .beads/beads.db .beads/beads.db.corrupt
-
-# Try repair
-sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "REINDEX;"
-sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "VACUUM;"
+# Let br preserve the DB family and rebuild only from valid JSONL
+br doctor --repair --json
 
-# Or rebuild from JSONL
-rm .beads/beads.db
-br sync --import-only
+# Verify the repaired workspace before writing
+br doctor --json
+br sync --status --json
 ```
 
 ---
@@ -696,9 +701,9 @@ cat .beads/beads.yaml | python3 -c "import yaml,sys; yaml.safe_load(sys.stdin)"
 # Find config paths
 br config path
 
-# Reset to defaults
-rm .beads/beads.yaml
-br init
+# Repair the YAML in place, then verify the effective config
+$EDITOR .beads/beads.yaml
+br config list -v
 ```
 
 ---
@@ -841,8 +846,9 @@ br list --limit 50
 # Use specific filters
 br list -s open -t bug
 
-# Vacuum database
-sqlite3 .beads/beads.db "VACUUM;"
+# Check whether derived-state repair is needed
+br doctor --json
+br doctor --repair --dry-run --json
 ```
 
 ---
@@ -939,83 +945,114 @@ br --actor "my-agent" update bd-123 --claim
 
 ## Recovery Procedures
 
-### Complete workspace recovery from JSONL
+Recovery starts with classification and evidence preservation. Do not delete,
+rename, or partially overwrite `.beads/` files as a first response. The repair
+path should either prove that JSONL is authoritative and rebuild from it, or
+stop and preserve the evidence needed for manual repair.
 
-```bash
-# Backup current state
-mv .beads .beads.backup
-
-# Reinitialize
-br init --prefix <your-prefix>
+### Capture the incident bundle
 
-# Import from JSONL
-cp .beads.backup/issues.jsonl .beads/
-br sync --import-only
+Run these commands before attempting repair:
 
-# Verify
-br stats
-br doctor
+```bash
+br doctor --json
+br doctor --repair --dry-run --json
+br sync --status --json
+br where --json
+br config list -v
 ```
 
----
+Also preserve the failing command, exact stdout/stderr, `.beads/issues.jsonl`,
+`.beads/metadata.json`, directory listings for `.beads/`, `.beads/.br_recovery/`,
+and `.beads/.br_history/`, plus the presence and hashes of `beads.db`,
+`beads.db-wal`, `beads.db-shm`, and `beads.db-journal` when present.
+
+### Recoverable database family with valid JSONL
 
-### Recovery from corrupted database
+Use this when `br doctor` classifies the DB family as recoverable and JSONL is
+parseable, conflict-free, and pointed at the intended workspace.
 
 ```bash
-# 1. Backup everything
-cp -r .beads .beads.backup.$(date +%Y%m%d)
+# Preview what repair will do
+br doctor --repair --dry-run --json
+
+# Execute the supported repair path
+br doctor --repair --json
 
-# 2. Export what we can
-br sync --flush-only --error-policy best-effort || true
+# Verify health and freshness before mutating issues

+br sync --status --json

-# 3. Check JSONL integrity
-jq -c '.' .beads/issues.jsonl >/dev/null && echo "JSONL OK"
+Expected behavior: `br` preserves the original DB family under
+`.beads/.br_recovery/`, rebuilds from valid JSONL, and verifies the repaired
+workspace instead of asking the operator to remove individual database files.
 
-# 4. Rebuild database
-rm .beads/beads.db
-br sync --import-only
+### Unsafe JSONL or merge-conflict state
 
-# 5. Verify
-br doctor
-br stats
+Use this when JSONL contains conflict markers, malformed lines, mixed prefixes,
+or data from the wrong workspace. Automatic import/rebuild is not safe until the
+interchange file is corrected.
+
+```bash
+# Find conflict markers or invalid JSONL
+grep -n "^<<<<<<\|^======\|^>>>>>>" .beads/issues.jsonl
+jq -c '.' .beads/issues.jsonl >/dev/null
+
+# Edit the JSONL to one valid record per line, with the intended prefix
+$EDITOR .beads/issues.jsonl
+
+# Re-validate and import after the file is unambiguous
+jq -c '.' .beads/issues.jsonl >/dev/null
+br sync --import-only --json
+br doctor --json
 ```
 
----
+Do not use raw `git checkout --ours` or `git checkout --theirs` as a recovery
+shortcut unless the operator has already decided which side is authoritative and
+has accepted the data-loss consequence. The safer default is line-level conflict
+resolution followed by import.
 
-### Recovery from git merge conflicts
+### SQLite/JSONL drift authority decision
 
-```bash
-# 1. Identify conflicts
-grep -l "<<<<<<" .beads/*.jsonl
+Use this when both sides are readable but disagree.
 
-# 2. Resolve manually or use ours/theirs
-git checkout --ours .beads/issues.jsonl
-# OR
-git checkout --theirs .beads/issues.jsonl
+```bash
+# Classify freshness and path metadata first
+br sync --status --json
+br where --json
+br config list -v
 
-# 3. Import resolved file
+# If JSONL is authoritative
 br sync --import-only --force
 
-# 4. Mark resolved
-git add .beads/issues.jsonl
+# If SQLite is authoritative
+br sync --flush-only --force
+
+# Verify after the chosen direction succeeds
+br sync --status --json
+br doctor --json
 ```
 
----
+The important part is the explicit direction choice. Do not run a force flag as
+a generic fix; it is an assertion about which side should win.
 
-### Emergency database reset
+### JSONL-only emergency read path
 
-**Warning:** This loses any changes not in JSONL.
+`--no-db` is useful when the SQLite family is unavailable and the operator needs
+to inspect valid JSONL, but it is not the normal recovery path for writes.
 
 ```bash
-# Nuclear option
-rm .beads/beads.db
-br sync --import-only
-
-# Verify nothing lost
-br stats
-br list --limit 0 | wc -l
+br --no-db list --json
+br --no-db show <issue-id> --json
+br --no-db ready --json
 ```
 
+After inspection, return to the supported repair/import/export flows above.
+Avoid repeating JSONL-only writes after a partial failure unless the previous
+command result has been reconciled against the JSONL contents.
